differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) with lupus nephritis and secondary antiphospholipid syndrome
Primary Antiphospholipid Syndrome (APS) with catastrophic APS (CAPS)
Membranous nephropathy with secondary hypercoagulability (renal vein thrombosis)
ANCA-associated vasculitis with pulmonary involvement and renal disease
Infectious pneumonia with secondary hypercoagulable state
next_steps: Perform diagnostic serologic workup: ANA, anti-dsDNA, anti-Smith, C3, C4, anticardiolipin antibodies, anti-beta2-glycoprotein I, and lupus anticoagulant
Obtain renal biopsy to establish histological diagnosis and guide immunosuppressive therapy
Perform echocardiogram to evaluate for cardiac sources of emboli or valvular vegetations (Libman-Sacks endocarditis)
Consider early initiation of high-dose corticosteroids if systemic autoimmune etiology is strongly suspected
Monitor renal function, electrolytes, and oxygenation status closely
Evaluate for underlying malignancy or chronic infection if systemic autoimmune serologies are negative
